Ms. Moon stated that the petition was submitted for e-filing and is pending. The Court stated unless there was an issue with the filing that Ms. Moon should receive a hearing date for the petition. Ms. Moon stated that they could amend the petition, that her client could resign, and that they could leave the final distribution to be dealt with by the successor. Mr. Lee argued in support of the removal of Cherie Estrada stating that it is two months later, that there is a document pending in e-filing, that they would like to take counsel up on her offer that her client resign, that they have concerns that some proceeds in the amount of $35,000 and/or $40,000 were not distributed equally, that there has been no cooperation to get this filed, and that the Court should grant their petition to take over this matter. Ms. Moon stated that she never received a copy of the petition for a successor. Mr. Lee stated that it was filed November 18, 2022, as an amendment to their initial petition. The Court stated that it was not inclined to replace the administrator, that it had taken a look at the document pending in the e-filing queue, that the document appears to be a substantial filing, that it hopes that this filing can be a path forward, and that it would keep Mr. Lee's petition for removal open. The clerk confirmed that Ms. Moon's petition was filed today with a hearing date of April 19, 2023.

At the conclusion of argument/comments, the Petition for Removal of Personal Representative and for Appointment of Successor Personal Representative is continued to April 19, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.
